title: Jal Mahal
tagline: Jal Mahal — heritage city of Rajasthan

overview.short: Jal Mahal (meaning "Water Palace") is a palace in the middle of the Man Sagar Lake in Jaipur city, the capital of the state of Rajasthan, India.
overview.description: Jal Mahal (meaning "Water Palace") is a palace in the middle of the Man Sagar Lake in Jaipur city, the capital of the state of Rajasthan, India. The palace was originally constructed around 1699; the building and the lake around it were later renovated and enlarged in the early 18th century by Maharaja Jai Singh II of Amber.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Man Sagar Lake
topPlaces.description: Man Sagar Lake is an artificial lake, situated in Jaipur, the capital city of the state of Rajasthan in India. It is named after Raja Man Singh, the then ruler of Amer, who constructed it in c. 1610 by damming the Dravyavati river.

topPlaces.name: Kanak Vrindavan
topPlaces.description: Kanak Vrindavan is a garden in Jaipur, the capital of Rajasthan. It is built in a valley surrounded by the Aravali Hills and is located on the way to the Amer Fort at the bottom of the Nahargarh hill.

topPlaces.name: Khole Ke Hanuman Ji Temple
topPlaces.description: Khole Ke Hanuman Ji Temple is a Hindu temple located at a distance of about 9 km from district headquarters of Jaipur, the capital of Rajasthan state in India. The temple is dedicated to a major Hindu deity Hanuman.

topPlaces.name: Garh Ganesh Temple
topPlaces.description: Garh Ganesh Temple is a Hindu temple located on the Aravalli hills near Jaipur, Rajasthan, India. It is dedicated to Lord Ganesha and is known for its unique idol, which does not feature a trunk-an uncommon representation of the deity.

topPlaces.name: The Raj Palace
topPlaces.description: The Raj Palace in Jaipur Rajasthan, India is more than two and a half centuries old palace converted into a grand heritage hotel. The hotel is awarded as "the best heritage hotel of India" by the Government of India and voted as "The leading heritage hotel of the world" consecutively for seven times by World Travel Awards.

topPlaces.name: Princess Diya Kumari Foundation
topPlaces.description: The Princess Diya Kumari Foundation (commonly abbreviated PDKF) is a Jaipur-based charity organisation named after Diya Kumari.

topPlaces.name: Maota Lake
topPlaces.description: Maota Lake is located in Amber, in the Indian state of Rajasthan. The ancient name of the lake is "Mahavata", but it is commonly known as Maota due to the large trees located near the lake.

topPlaces.name: Govind Dev Ji Temple
topPlaces.description: The Govind Dev Ji Temple is a significant Hindu temple dedicated to Radha and Krishna, located within the City Palace complex, in Jaipur, Rajasthan, India. The temple was established in the 18th century by Maharaja Jai Singh II, the founder of Jaipur, who brought the deities from Vrindavan.

items.note: Man Sagar Lake is an artificial lake, situated in Jaipur, the capital city of the state of Rajasthan in India. It is named after Raja Man Singh, the then ruler of Amer, who constructed it in c. 1610 by damming the Dravyavati river.

Kanak Vrindavan is a garden in Jaipur, the capital of Rajasthan. It is built in a valley surrounded by the Aravali Hills and is located on the way to the Amer Fort at the bottom of the Nahargarh hill.

Khole Ke Hanuman Ji Temple is a Hindu temple located at a distance of about 9 km from district headquarters of Jaipur, the capital of Rajasthan state in India. The temple is dedicated to a major Hindu deity Hanuman.

Garh Ganesh Temple is a Hindu temple located on the Aravalli hills near Jaipur, Rajasthan, India. It is dedicated to Lord Ganesha and is known for its unique idol, which does not feature a trunk-an uncommon representation of the deity.

The Raj Palace in Jaipur Rajasthan, India is more than two and a half centuries old palace converted into a grand heritage hotel. The hotel is awarded as "the best heritage hotel of India" by the Government of India and voted as "The leading heritage hotel of the world" consecutively for seven times by World Travel Awards.
The Princess Diya Kumari Foundation (commonly abbreviated PDKF) is a Jaipur-based charity organisation named after Diya Kumari.
Maota Lake is located in Amber, in the Indian state of Rajasthan. The ancient name of the lake is "Mahavata", but it is commonly known as Maota due to the large trees located near the lake.

The Govind Dev Ji Temple is a significant Hindu temple dedicated to Radha and Krishna, located within the City Palace complex, in Jaipur, Rajasthan, India. The temple was established in the 18th century by Maharaja Jai Singh II, the founder of Jaipur, who brought the deities from Vrindavan.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jal Mahal?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jal Mahal is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jal Mahal?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jaipur International Airport (15 km) and the nearest railway station is Jaipur Junction (7 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jal Mahal?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jal Mahal at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jal Mahal?
faq.a: Stays in Jal Mahal start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jal Mahal?
faq.a: The top attractions are Man Sagar Lake, Kanak Vrindavan, Khole Ke Hanuman Ji Temple, Garh Ganesh Temple, The Raj Palace.
